mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns despite PAYEMS increasing by > 100k in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which signals economic resilience and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite INDPRO increasing by > 2% in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which drives economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite HOUST increasing by > 5% in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary policy to combat inflation, which destabilizes equity markets and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market tightness, which may force central banks to raise rates, creating headwinds for equity markets and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns despite UNRATE falling > 0.3pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated consumer demand, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite RSAFS increasing by > 2% in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth (GDPC1) signals economic stagnation, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns despite GDPC1 growth remaining below 1% for 3 months.
